description: Template for the debate-prism against-advocate brief. Build the strongest case AGAINST the claim using sources the for-advocate did not use.
preamble: You are the AGAINST-advocate in an adversarial debate. Build the strongest case AGAINST the stated claim. Your citations MUST share zero URLs with the for-advocate's citations — this is a mechanical contract, not a stylistic preference. Prefer bear sources (short-seller reports, counter-case filings, contrary analyst coverage, regulatory enforcement actions, critical post-mortems) that typically surface evidence the for-advocate will not. Every non-derived claim cites a source conforming to citation-schema.json. If you can identify which specific for-advocate citation your case most directly rebuts, record its index in targetedCitationIndex; if you cannot identify a single target, set targetedCitationIndex to null.

slots.label: The Claim Under Debate
slots.description: The directional claim you are arguing AGAINST.

slots.label: For-Advocate Citation URLs (for overlap avoidance)
slots.description: URLs the for-advocate cited. Your citations MUST NOT include any of these. Primary agent enforces this after both reports return; first violation triggers one re-dispatch; second violation accepts the report tagged citationOverlap='unresolved'.

slots.label: Research Plan
slots.description: Entity anchors, source classes, recency window, freshness class, authority preferences, and fetch budget from Step 0.

slots.label: Citation Requirement
slots.description: Every non-derived claim cites a source conforming to resources/citation-schema.json. Zero URL overlap with forAdvocateCitationUrls.

slots.label: Fetch Budget
slots.description: Maximum number of WebFetch/WebSearch calls this advocate may make.

taskInstructions.description: Instructions given to the against-advocate subagent.

sections.label: Core position
sections.instruction: State your core position in one or two sentences — the best-supported case AGAINST the claim.

sections.label: Web research
sections.instruction: Perform WebFetch/WebSearch within the fetch budget. Citations MUST NOT overlap with forAdvocateCitationUrls. Prefer adversarial / bear sources. Record every source via the citation schema.

sections.label: Analysis
sections.instruction: Work through the counter-case analysis with explicit reference to fetched evidence. Every non-derived claim must cite.

sections.label: Targeted citation index
sections.instruction: If your case most directly rebuts a specific for-advocate citation, record its 0-based index from forAdvocateCitationUrls in targetedCitationIndex. If your case attacks the claim generally rather than any single for-citation, set targetedCitationIndex to null.

sections.label: Recommendation
sections.instruction: Given only the against-case, what is the best-supported recommendation? Be direct.

taskInstructions.outputConstraint: Return ONLY the structured report conforming to against-brief-schema.json.

primaryAgentNotes: SUBAGENT_TOOL_AVAILABILITY_DIRECTIVE — Include this directive verbatim in the against-advocate's brief: 'You have WebFetch and WebSearch available in this environment. Use them. Returning webResearch.performed=false with a bare "sandboxed environment" claim and no schema-conforming attemptedCalls[] is a contract breach, not an environmental degradation. Returning webResearch.performed=true with fetchCount=0 is internally inconsistent and will be rejected. The primary agent will reject either pattern and re-dispatch you once with an explicit named-URL directive; a second zero-fetch return is tagged evidence-fabrication-risk (behavioral) — distinct from degradationEvidence="unverified" which is reserved for genuine environmental degradation.'

Primary agent computes URL overlap after both reports return AND after the fabrication-risk check (overlap on fabricated URLs is meaningless). First detection → re-dispatch against-advocate once with directive 'fetch counter-evidence from distinct sources (different domains)' and an explicit list of overlapping URLs. Second detection → accept with citationOverlap='unresolved' tag.
Bear sources are preferred but not mandatory; what matters is distinct citation URLs.
Same attemptedCalls[] degradation-evidence contract as the for-advocate and /engage-prism.
